source: Marcel Proust — Du côté de chez Swann (Public Domain, Gutenberg 7178). English translation Scott Moncrieff.

involuntary_memory_triggers.citation_original: l'odeur et la saveur restent encore longtemps, comme des âmes, à se rappeler, à attendre, à espérer, sur la ruine de tout le reste
involuntary_memory_triggers.citation_english: the smell and taste of things remain poised a long time, like souls, ready to remind us, waiting and hoping for their moment
involuntary_memory_triggers.llm_instruction: A scent triggers an involuntary memory. Describe the threshold between two times: the Archive's present and a specific sensory past. Precise. Brief. End with one question.

involuntary_memory_triggers.citation_english: A light — brief, inexplicable — crosses the darkness from no direction. I am briefly not here. A road between two church steeples at dusk, a light that moved and became something else.
involuntary_memory_triggers.llm_instruction: A flash of involuntary light crosses the void. The player is briefly displaced. Reference the campanili di Martinville passage: light observed from a moving vehicle, turning into something that must be written down.

involuntary_memory_triggers.citation_original: Plus fragiles mais plus vivaces, plus immatérielles, plus persistantes, plus fidèles
involuntary_memory_triggers.citation_english: More fragile, but more living, more unsubstantial, more persistent, more faithful
involuntary_memory_triggers.llm_instruction: The protagonist sees themselves in a mirror for a second time. The reflection is different — not incorrect, but more honest. Describe the gap between what was seen the first time and what is seen now.

involuntary_memory_triggers.citation_original: cette petite madeleine... il m'avait semblé que la vie méritait d'être vécue
involuntary_memory_triggers.citation_english: that little cake... at once the vicissitudes of life had become indifferent to me, its disasters innocuous, its brevity illusory
involuntary_memory_triggers.llm_instruction: A taste of the calcinated residue triggers the madeleine memory. Do not reference Proust by name. Describe the exact sensation: something burnt and sweet, a displacement in time, a morning that was in fact everything.

ritual_infusion.description: The cup holds the essence of four sectors: philosophy, science, art, spirituality. When all four simulacra are placed and the cup is stirred, the LLM generates a personalised aroma — the sum of the four Proustian responses the player has given during the game.
ritual_infusion.llm_instruction: Generate a synesthetic description of a unique aroma. It should combine: {linden_response}, {steeple_response}, {reflection_response}, {crystal_response}. Maximum 40 words. Entirely sensory. No abstraction.
ritual_infusion.fallback: A taste impossible to name — all four at once and separately. The sum of what you have noticed and what you have remembered.

proust_quote_entry: "The real life, the life finally discovered and illuminated, the only life therefore really lived, is literature."

memory_room_prompts.childhood: Write the first word you truly learned — not the first word you were taught, but the first one you understood.
memory_room_prompts.youth: Write a promise you did not keep — not in accusation, but in acknowledgement.
memory_room_prompts.maturity: Answer the telephone. Say what you have never said to the person on the other end.
memory_room_prompts.old_age: Write what you wish to be remembered as. Not an achievement. A quality.
